_doc: System reflection memory (TradingAgents memory.py style). situation->lesson, retrieved by BM25 similarity at each review/decision and injected so the system learns. Write hook (add/save) ready; v1 = seeded + manual append until closed-trade postmortems exist.

lessons.situation: A holding is down sharply after an earnings report, trading below its 50-day and 200-day moving averages in a downtrend, and I am tempted to add more to lower my average cost.
lessons.lesson: Do NOT average down into a post-earnings breakdown without a fresh fundamental reason. ORCL was bought 250->230->214->202 into a -47%-off-high slide. Set a stop/review level; hold or cut per thesis, never add to a downtrend just to lower cost.

lessons.situation: A leading sub-theme's ETF is 60-80% above its 200-day moving average and most constituent names are sitting at 52-week highs after a big run.
lessons.lesson: Don't chase parabolic leaders; the theme is overheated/late-stage. Wait for a pullback or base; favor less-extended quality leaders or supply-chain chokepoints in a healthy pullback. (SMH +60% / SOXX +78% over 200d on 6/18; MU/SNDK/ALAB parabolic.)

lessons.situation: Considering a large, well-covered mega-cap AI leader that is expensive on every valuation multiple but is the obvious main-line name everyone owns.
lessons.lesson: It's a momentum/quality HOLD, not a Serenity asymmetric chokepoint - cognitive-gap alpha is low because it's the obvious shovel seller. Size per vol x correlation cap; don't expect microcap-style asymmetry. (AVGO: DCF -52%, EV/EBITDA 47x, but ROE 36%, main-line leader.)

lessons.situation: I want to add a second semiconductor name alongside an existing semiconductor position.
lessons.lesson: Check PAIRWISE correlation, not just average. NVDA-TSM ~0.74 over 6 weeks. Adding highly-correlated semis concentrates risk even if the average-correlation cap looks fine; let the correlation multiplier shrink the combined cap, and net new semis against existing NVDA exposure.

lessons.situation: A high-growth small-cap supply-chain name has a large active ATM (at-the-market offering) and/or heavy stock-based compensation.
lessons.lesson: Treat a large active ATM + SBC as a structural disqualifier / price ceiling (Serenity #7): retail equity gets diluted to fund the buildout. Check SEC EDGAR for dilution terms. (IREN $6B ATM ~= 51% overhang -> explicit bear.)

lessons.situation: Evaluating an early-stage / qualification-phase supply-chain name on its trailing TTM revenue and earnings.
lessons.lesson: For qualification-stage names, model FORWARD TAM at the architectural inflection, not TTM revenue (Serenity #6: qualification precedes revenue). But cross-validate forward/contract claims against SEC EDGAR / 10-K and flag as to-verify before sizing up.
